title: Pentagon Seeks Additional $200 Billion to Fund Iran War
summary: The request, which the White House has not submitted to Congress, is already encountering some resistance.

title: Attacks on Oil and Natural Gas Facilities Could Lead to Much Higher Prices
summary: Attacks on oil and natural gas facilities this week could make it much harder for Persian Gulf countries to rebuild and restart production when the war eventually end.

title: The Effects of High Oil Prices
summary: Our chief economics correspondent, Ben Casselman, breaks down how gasoline prices have responded to the oil crisis in the Persian Gulf, and what is in store for inflation if the price of oil remains above $100 per barrel.

title: ‘Go Big and Go Loud’: Inside the Justice Dept.’s Push to Prosecute Protesters
summary: Prosecutors have struggled to prove in court what the president and his aides have repeatedly said in public: that a network of leftist activists presents a serious threat to national security.

title: Trump Jokes About Pearl Harbor in Meeting With Japan’s Leader
summary: Breaking a taboo, President Trump needled Japan’s prime minister about the World War II attack, as she widened her eyes and appeared to take a deep breath in the Oval Office.

title: Lawsuit Accuses Justice Dept. Leadership of ‘Political Retribution’
summary: The suit filed by two fired F.B.I. agents aimed to hold the Trump administration accountable for the purge of personnel who had worked on the investigations of Mr. Trump or his allies.

title: ‘We’re Just Seen as Sex Objects’: Dolores Huerta’s Years in the U.F.W.
summary: The co-founder of the United Farm Workers talked about her relationship with Cesar Chavez, and the night he raped her.

title: California to Rename Chavez Holiday as ‘Farmworkers Day’
summary: Some states and cities have canceled their observances of Cesar Chavez Day on March 31 after revelations that he abused girls and women.

title: Cesar Chavez, a Civil Rights Icon, Is Accused of Abusing Girls for Years
summary: An investigation by The New York Times found extensive evidence that the United Farm Workers co-founder groomed and sexually abused girls who worked in the movement.

title: How Did Flea Make a Jazz Album? Practice, Practice, Practice.
summary: The Red Hot Chili Peppers bassist returned to the trumpet, for a new record featuring Nick Cave, Thom Yorke and a core cast of contemporary jazz luminaries.

title: 24 States Sue E.P.A. Over Climate Change Decision
summary: The suit accuses the agency of illegally repealing the endangerment finding, the scientific assessment that required it to regulate greenhouse gases.

title: A Gift From Trump to the Supreme Court
summary: In a caustic critique of the court issued on social media late Sunday night, the president inadvertently buttressed its independence.

title: Iran War Narrows Fed’s Path to Rate Cuts as Trump Demands Immediate Relief
summary: Jerome H. Powell, the Fed chair, wants to keep the central bank’s policy options open as officials stare down another economic shock that threatens to stoke inflation and crimp growth.

title: Mortgage Rates Highest in Three Months as War Weighs on Housing Market
summary: Rising energy costs and concerns about inflation have helped lift borrowing costs for a home mortgage for the third consecutive week.

title: Banking Regulators Prepare to Loosen Post-Crisis Capital Rules
summary: The changes, long sought by bank lobbyists, would reduce the capital banks must hold in reserve to mitigate risks.

title: I Taught My Son Everything, Except How to Take a Vacation
summary: He was about to leave for college when I realized: I had never taken him on a real vacation.

title: Boston Scientific Knew of Pacemaker Battery Problems for Years
summary: Boston Scientific announced multiple recalls but has said its battery issues were limited. One internal test in 2025 found batteries with an “extremely high failure rate.”

title: The Long Farewell to Mark Zuckerberg’s Metaverse
summary: Meta announced changes that effectively leave Mr. Zuckerberg’s vision of an immersive digital world based in virtual reality only on life support.

title: Elon Musk Whips Up Frenzy Against John Thune Over SAVE America Act
summary: Elon Musk is furiously pushing for Senator John Thune, the majority leader, to pass a strict voter ID bill. But so far, he hasn’t publicly spent money to promote the legislation.

title: What a Key AIPAC Loss in Illinois Signals for Pro-Israel Lobby in Midterms
summary: The historically bipartisan American Israel Public Affairs Committee faces headwinds among Democrats angered by the country’s current government and war in Gaza.

title: The Wealthy House Candidate With a History of Bawdy Facebook Posts
summary: The social media habits of Peter Chatzky, a tech executive who is running to unseat Representative Mike Lawler in New York, show a penchant for crude jokes.

title: She Killed a Family With Her Speeding Car. Is Probation Enough?
summary: Two years ago, an older driver killed a couple, their toddler and their baby as her vehicle sped through San Francisco. A judge has indicated that he intends to let her avoid prison, home detention and community service.

title: New Museum Debuts New Building With Ambitious ‘New Humans’
summary: It’s a big, serious, adult show worth debating and even fighting over — just the way our critic likes it.

title: Trump Is Putting His Stamp on the World
summary: Be careful what you wish for.

title: White Identity Is Galvanizing the Right
summary: He wrote a book on anti-white bias. The White House noticed.

title: How Immigration Became an Issue in Argentina
summary: Argentina has often stood out for its openness to immigration. Under President Javier Milei, it has started to crack down.

title: The Soldier Who Came Back From the Dead
summary: Told that Nazar Daletskyi had died, his Ukrainian family buried what they thought were his remains. He turned up three years later in a prisoner-of-war exchange.

title: Uber to Buy Rivian Vehicles for Use as Driverless Taxis
summary: The deal provides cash to Rivian and is part of Uber’s attempts to stay relevant in a driverless future.

title: Home Health Care Aides Say It’s Time to End ‘Inhumane’ 24-Hour Shifts
summary: The aides want the New York City Council to pass a law that would limit their shifts to 12 hours, except in the case of emergencies.
